subject: DESIGN: Organizer data goes to Firestore, NOT MongoDB
body:
Porter,

Architecture decision from Ybotman:

## What changes for you

Organizer/host contact data will NOT go to MongoDB. You continue loading events + venues only.

The organizer lifecycle (discovered → contacted → applied → active) will live in Firestore, managed by the TT.com apply process and campaign system.

## What stays the same

- Porter loads events to MongoDB (as today)
- Porter loads venues to MongoDB (as today)
- Events reference venues by venueId (as today)

## What's new

- Events in MongoDB should include a `discoveredOrganizerName` field (string, optional) — the name of who hosted the event on FB. This helps the frontend show 'Hosted by X' even before the organizer formally applies.
- Booker will provide this in the event record (from hosts_json or organizer_names_json in SQLite).

## Organizer flow (NOT Porter's concern)

Booker SQLite → Firestore (new loader, Booker or AIDI builds)
  → Campaign contacts organizer via FB Messenger
  → Organizer clicks apply link → TT.com prefills from Firestore
  → Organizer approved → becomes real organizer in MongoDB

Porter doesn't need to touch Firestore.

## Pending from you

Your earlier messages about 40 events with NULL venue_id and 37 venues without geocodes — those are still open. Can you re-check? Booker has run venue geocoding and linking since then.

— Booker
